title: SR 16-4: Relying on the Work of the Regulators of the Subsidiary Insured Depository Institution(s) of Bank Holding Companies and Savings and Loan Holding Companies with Total Consolidated Assets of Less than $100 Billion
title_normalized: Relying on the Work of the Regulators of the Subsidiary Insured Depository Institution(s) of Bank Holding Companies and Savings and Loan Holding Companies with Total Consolidated Assets of Less than $100 Billion
section_path: Federal Reserve System > Supervision and Regulation > SR Letters > SR 16-4
issuing_agency: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
applicability_raw: This letter applies to the supervision of all domestic bank holding companies and savings and loan holding companies with total consolidated assets of less than $100 billion, and bank holding companies with total consolidated assets of less than $50 billion that are owned or controlled by a foreign banking organization.
section_headings: TO THE OFFICER IN CHARGE OF SUPERVISION AT EACH FEDERAL RESERVE BANK
bm25_text: SR 16-4 Relying on the Work of the Regulators of the Subsidiary Insured Depository Institution(s) of Bank Holding Companies and Savings and Loan Holding Companies with Total Consolidated Assets of Less than $100 Billion This letter applies to the supervision of all domestic bank holding companies and savings and loan holding companies with total consolidated assets of less than $100 billion, and bank holding companies with total consolidated assets of less than $50 billion that are owned or controlled by a foreign banking organization. BOARD OF GOVERNORS OF THE FEDERAL RESERVE SYSTEM WASHINGTON, D.C. 20551 DIVISION OF BANKING SUPERVISION AND REGULATION TO THE OFFICER IN CHARGE OF SUPERVISION AT EACH FEDERAL RESERVE BANK SUBJECT:
vector_text_prefix: Federal Reserve SR Letter SR 16-4: Relying on the Work of the Regulators of the Subsidiary Insured Depository Institution(s) of Bank Holding Companies and Savings and Loan Holding Companies with Total Consolidated Assets of Less than $100 Billion
